title: Delicate Vietnamese Chicken Spring Rolls
description: A Whole30-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring crumbly spring rolls with an earthy flavor, perfect for a date night, made with egg wash and lacquer-glaze, these rolls are a delight to eat.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the egg and a pinch of salt.
Add the shredded carrots and zucchini to the bowl and mix well.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and mix until just combined.
Fill a large bowl with warm water.
Place a rice paper wrapper into the water for 10-15 seconds, until it becomes soft and pliable.
Remove the wrapper from the water and place it on a clean surface.
Place about 1/4 cup of the chicken mixture onto the center of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the spring rolls to the skillet and cook until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Brush the lacquer glaze over the spring rolls during the last minute of cooking.
